src: Ἡ μὲν γραφὴ τῆς ἑβραϊκῆς ᾿Εξόδου ἀνέγνωσται
lit: The scripture of the Hebrew Exodus has on the one hand been read.
sm: The passage from the Hebrew Exodus has been read aloud.
ana: Ἡ μὲν γραφὴ τῆς ἑβραϊκῆς Ἐξόδου ἀνέγνωσται
emd: Normalized spacing/breathing in ᾿Εξόδου -> Ἐξόδου for analysis.

syn.mc: Ἡ ... γραφὴ ... ἀνέγνωσται.
syn.sub: No finite subordinate clause; the genitive phrase τῆς ἑβραϊκῆς Ἐξόδου depends on γραφή.
syn.svo: Subject: γραφή. Verb: ἀνέγνωσται. No direct object.
syn.rhet: μὲν anticipates the balancing development in the following lines.

src: καὶ τὰ ῥήματα τοῦ μυστηρίου διασεσάφηται,
lit: And the words of the mystery have been made clear.
sm: And the words of the mystery have been explained.

syn.mc: καὶ τὰ ῥήματα τοῦ μυστηρίου διασεσάφηται.
syn.sub: No subordinate clause; τοῦ μυστηρίου is a dependent genitive modifying ῥήματα.
syn.svo: Subject: τὰ ῥήματα. Verb: διασεσάφηται. No direct object.
syn.rhet: This line balances line 1: scripture has been read, and its meaning has been clarified.

lit: How the sheep is sacrificed.
sm: How the sacrificial lamb is offered.

syn.mc: Finite clause dependent on the sense of explanation from lines 1-2.
syn.sub: The clause is an indirect explanatory clause introduced by πῶς, dependent on διασεσάφηται.
syn.svo: Subject: τὸ πρόβατον. Verb: θύεται. No direct object.
syn.rhet: First member of a threefold πῶς-sequence continued in lines 4-5.

lit: And how the people is saved.
sm: And how the people are saved.
emd: Followed change_log.txt: ὃ -> ὁ (article, not relative pronoun).

syn.mc: Coordinate explanatory clause.
syn.sub: Like line 3, this πῶς-clause depends on the preceding statement that the mystery has been clarified.
syn.svo: Subject: ὁ λαός. Verb: σώζεται. No direct object.
syn.rhet: Second member of the repeated πῶς-anaphora.

src: καὶ πῶς ὁ Φαραὼ διὰ τοῦ μυστηρίου μαστίζεται.
lit: And how Pharaoh is scourged through the mystery.
sm: And how Pharaoh is smitten through the mystery.

syn.mc: Coordinate explanatory clause completing the threefold sequence.
syn.sub: πῶς introduces another indirect explanatory clause.
syn.svo: Subject: ὁ Φαραώ. Verb: μαστίζεται. Prepositional phrase: διὰ τοῦ μυστηρίου expresses instrumentality or means.
syn.rhet: Climactic third member of the πῶς-series: sacrifice, salvation, judgment.

lit: Therefore understand, O beloved ones,
sm: So then, understand, beloved friends,

syn.mc: Direct imperative clause.
syn.sub: The imperative governs the ὅπως-clause that begins in line 7.
syn.svo: Implied subject: ὑμεῖς. Verb: ξύνετε. Direct address: ὦ ἀγαπητοί.
syn.rhet: Transition from exposition to exhortation.
syn.app: The adjective ἀγαπητοί functions substantivally in vocative address.

lit: How it is new and old,
sm: How it is both new and old,

syn.mc: Finite clause dependent on ξύνετε.
syn.sub: ὅπως introduces the content to be understood; the grammatical subject is delayed until line 10.
syn.svo: Verb: ἐστίν. Delayed subject: τὸ ... μυστήριον (line 10). Predicate adjectives: καινὸν, παλαιόν.
syn.rhet: Beginning of an antithetical series of balanced predicate adjectives.

lit: Everlasting and temporary,
sm: both everlasting and temporary,

syn.mc: Elliptical continuation of the clause begun in line 7.
syn.sub: Still within the ὅπως-clause dependent on ξύνετε.
syn.svo: No expressed verb in the line; ἐστίν is carried over from line 7. Delayed subject remains τὸ ... μυστήριον in line 10.
syn.rhet: Second antithetical pair in the catalog.

lit: Corruptible and incorruptible,
sm: both corruptible and incorruptible,

syn.mc: Elliptical continuation of the same delayed-subject clause.
syn.sub: Still governed by ὅπως ἐστίν from line 7.
syn.svo: Verb implied from line 7; subject delayed to line 10.
syn.rhet: Third antithetical pair, intensifying the paradox.

src: θνητὸν καὶ ἀθάνατον τὸ τοῦ πάσχα μυστήριον"
lit: Mortal and immortal, the mystery of the Passover.
sm: The mystery of the Passover is both mortal and immortal.
ana: θνητὸν καὶ ἀθάνατον τὸ τοῦ πάσχα μυστήριον·
emd: Following the correction policy used in change_log.txt, the trailing OCR quotation mark is normalized here as punctuation.

syn.mc: Completion of the delayed-subject clause begun in line 7: τὸ τοῦ πάσχα μυστήριον is the subject of ἐστίν.
syn.sub: No new subordinate clause; this line closes the ὅπως-clause dependent on ξύνετε.
syn.svo: Subject: τὸ τοῦ πάσχα μυστήριον. Verb: ἐστίν (understood from line 7). Predicate adjectives: θνητὸν, ἀθάνατον, along with the earlier pairs in lines 7-9.
syn.rhet: Fourth antithetical pair completes a tightly arranged tetrad: new/old, everlasting/temporary, corruptible/incorruptible, mortal/immortal.

lit: Old, on the one hand, according to the law,
sm: old, on the one hand, in relation to the law,

syn.mc: Elliptical continuation of the predication established in lines 7-10.
syn.sub: No new finite subordinate clause; the phrase continues the explanatory series about τὸ τοῦ πάσχα μυστήριον.
syn.svo: Implied subject: τὸ τοῦ πάσχα μυστήριον. Understood copula: ἐστίν. Predicate adjective: παλαιόν. Prepositional phrase: κατὰ τὸν νόμον marks respect or standard.
syn.rhet: First member of a fourfold explanatory antithesis unpacking line 10.

lit: But new [according to the] word,
sm: but new in relation to the Word/discourse,
emd: Trailing OCR quotation mark normalized to a comma in the continuing series; editorial brackets preserved.

syn.mc: Elliptical continuation paired antithetically with line 11.
syn.sub: No subordinate clause; the bracketed prepositional phrase restores the parallel structure with line 11.
syn.svo: Implied subject: τὸ τοῦ πάσχα μυστήριον. Understood copula: ἐστίν. Predicate adjective: καινόν. Prepositional phrase: [κατὰ τὸν] λόγον.
syn.rhet: Balanced antithesis to παλαιὸν μὲν ... νόμον.

lit: Temporary according to the type,
sm: temporary in respect of the type,

syn.mc: Elliptical continuation of the same explanatory series.
syn.sub: No subordinate clause; line 13 forms the first half of another antithetical pair, completed by line 14.
syn.svo: Implied subject: τὸ τοῦ πάσχα μυστήριον. Understood copula: ἐστίν. Predicate adjective: πρόσκαιρον. Prepositional phrase: κατὰ τὸν [τύπον].
syn.rhet: Pairs with line 14 as temporary/eternal, type/grace.

lit: Everlasting because of grace,
sm: but everlasting because of grace,
emd: Trailing OCR quotation mark normalized to a comma in the continuing series.

syn.mc: Elliptical continuation paired with line 13.
syn.sub: No subordinate clause; διὰ τὴν χάριν provides causal explanation.
syn.svo: Implied subject: τὸ τοῦ πάσχα μυστήριον. Understood copula: ἐστίν. Predicate adjective: ἀΐδιον. Prepositional phrase: διὰ τὴν χάριν expresses cause or basis.
syn.rhet: Antithetical answer to πρόσκαιρον κατὰ τὸν τύπον.

src: φθαρτ[ὸν διὰ τὴν] τοῦ προβάτου σφαγήν,
lit: Corruptible because of the slaughter of the sheep,
sm: corruptible because of the slaughter of the sacrificial lamb,
emd: Editorial brackets interrupt the phrase; analyzed lexically as φθαρτὸν διὰ τὴν τοῦ προβάτου σφαγήν.

syn.mc: Elliptical continuation of the predicative series.
syn.sub: No subordinate clause; the genitive τοῦ προβάτου depends on σφαγήν.
syn.svo: Implied subject: τὸ τοῦ πάσχα μυστήριον. Understood copula: ἐστίν. Predicate adjective: φθαρτόν. Causal phrase: διὰ τὴν τοῦ προβάτου σφαγήν.
syn.rhet: Forms the third explanatory antithesis with line 16.

lit: Incorruptible because of the life of the Lord,
sm: incorruptible because of the Lord's life,
emd: Trailing OCR quotation mark normalized to a comma; editorial brackets preserved.

syn.mc: Elliptical continuation paired with line 15.
syn.sub: No subordinate clause; τοῦ κυρίου is a dependent genitive with ζωήν.
syn.svo: Implied subject: τὸ τοῦ πάσχα μυστήριον. Understood copula: ἐστίν. Predicate adjective: ἄφθαρτον. Causal phrase: [διὰ τὴν] τοῦ κυρίου ζωήν.
syn.rhet: Antithetical completion of the corruptible/incorruptible pair.

lit: Mortal because of the burial [into earth],
sm: mortal because of the burial into the earth,

syn.mc: Elliptical continuation of the same explanatory pattern.
syn.sub: No subordinate clause; [εἰς γῆν] modifies ταφήν within the causal prepositional phrase.
syn.svo: Implied subject: τὸ τοῦ πάσχα μυστήριον. Understood copula: ἐστίν. Predicate adjective: θνητόν. Causal phrase: διὰ τὴν [εἰς γῆν] ταφήν.
syn.rhet: Prepares the mortal/immortal contrast completed in line 18.

lit: Immortal because of the resurrection out of [the] dead.
sm: immortal because of the resurrection from the dead.

syn.mc: Elliptical continuation paired with line 17.
syn.sub: No subordinate clause; ἐκ [νεκρῶν] modifies ἀνάστασιν within the causal phrase.
syn.svo: Implied subject: τὸ τοῦ πάσχα μυστήριον. Understood copula: ἐστίν. Predicate adjective: ἀθάνατον. Causal phrase: διὰ τὴν ἐκ [νεκρῶν] ἀνάστασιν.
syn.rhet: Closes the fourfold explanatory set begun in line 11.

lit: Old, on the one hand, the law,
sm: the law is old, on the one hand,
emd: Followed change_log.txt: ὃ -> ὁ (article, not relative pronoun).

syn.mc: Verbless nominal clause.
syn.sub: No subordinate clause; line 19 begins a fresh sequence of nominal antitheses corresponding to lines 11-18.
syn.svo: Subject: ὁ νόμος. Implied copula: ἐστίν. Predicate adjective: παλαιός.
syn.rhet: Nominal restatement of the previous abstract predicate pair.

lit: [New, but, the] word,
sm: but the word is new,
emd: Trailing OCR quotation mark normalized to a comma; editorial brackets preserved.

syn.mc: Verbless nominal clause paired with line 19.
syn.sub: No subordinate clause; the bracketed restoration supplies the expected predicate and article.
syn.svo: Subject: ὁ λόγος. Implied copula: ἐστίν. Predicate adjective: καινός.
syn.rhet: Balances παλαιὸς μὲν ὁ νόμος with a tersely antithetical nominal clause.

lit: Temporary the type,
sm: the type is temporary,
emd: Followed change_log.txt: ὃ -> ὁ (article, not relative pronoun).

syn.mc: Verbless nominal clause.
syn.sub: No subordinate clause; line 21 opens the next nominal contrast.
syn.svo: Subject: ὁ τύπος. Implied copula: ἐστίν. Predicate adjective: πρόσκαιρος.
syn.rhet: Pairs with line 22 as type/grace, temporary/eternal.

lit: But everlasting the grace,
sm: but grace is everlasting,
emd: Trailing OCR quotation mark normalized to a comma in the continuing nominal series.

syn.mc: Verbless nominal clause paired with line 21.
syn.sub: No subordinate clause.
syn.svo: Subject: ἡ χάρις. Implied copula: ἐστίν. Predicate adjective: ἀΐδιος.
syn.rhet: Completes the type/grace contrast in nominal form.

lit: Corruptible the sheep,
sm: the sheep is corruptible,
emd: Editorial brackets indicate partial restoration of φθαρτόν and of the phrase as a whole; analyzed lexically as φθαρτὸν τὸ πρόβατον.

syn.mc: Verbless nominal clause.
syn.sub: No subordinate clause; this line restates line 15 in concrete nominal form.
syn.svo: Subject: τὸ πρόβατον. Implied copula: ἐστίν. Predicate adjective: φθαρτόν.
syn.rhet: Pairs with line 24 as sheep/Lord, corruptible/incorruptible.

lit: Incorruptible the Lord,
sm: the Lord is incorruptible,
emd: Trailing OCR quotation mark normalized to a comma in the continuing nominal series.

syn.mc: Verbless nominal clause paired with line 23.
syn.sub: No subordinate clause.
syn.svo: Subject: ὁ κύριος. Implied copula: ἐστίν. Predicate adjective: ἄφθαρτος.
syn.rhet: Closes the sequence law/word, type/grace, sheep/Lord.

tok: aor pass ptcp nom sg m
lit: Not having been broken, like a lamb,
sm: unbroken, like a lamb,
emd: Stray OCR quotation mark removed for readability; no lexical change.

syn.mc: Participial phrase without an expressed finite verb.
syn.sub: No subordinate finite clause; the phrase modifies the implicit christological subject carried over from ὁ κύριος.
syn.svo: Implicit referent: ὁ κύριος / Christ. Participle: συντριβεὶς, negated by μή. Comparative phrase: ὡς ἀμνός.
syn.rhet: Pairs with line 27: lamb/God, unbroken/raised.
syn.ptc: συντριβεὶς is circumstantial/descriptive, evoking the paschal lamb whose bones are not broken.

tok: aor pass ptcp nom sg m
lit: But having been raised, as God.
sm: but raised up as God.

syn.mc: Participial phrase coordinated with line 26.
syn.sub: No subordinate finite clause; δέ marks contrast within the pair.
syn.svo: Implicit referent: ὁ κύριος / Christ. Participle: ἀνασταθείς. Comparative or predicative phrase: ὡς θεός.
syn.rhet: Counterbalances line 26 in a sharp lamb/God antithesis.
syn.ptc: ἀνασταθείς is circumstantial/descriptive, contrasting resurrection glory with sacrificial vulnerability.
syn.app: θεός stands in predicative relation to the implied subject under ὡς.

src: εἰ καὶ γὰρ ὡς πρόβατον εἰς σφαγὴν ἤχθη."
lit: For even if as a sheep he was led to slaughter.
sm: For even if he was led to slaughter like a sheep.
ana: εἰ καὶ γὰρ ὡς πρόβατον εἰς σφαγὴν ἤχθη.
emd: Stray OCR quotation mark before the period removed; no lexical change.

syn.mc: Concessive clause preparing the contrast in line 29.
syn.sub: εἰ καὶ introduces a concessive clause; γάρ gives explanatory force to the concession.
syn.svo: Implicit subject: Christ. Verb: ἤχθη. Comparative predicate: ὡς πρόβατον. Prepositional phrase: εἰς σφαγήν.
syn.rhet: Introduces the sheep/lamb imagery that is immediately qualified and denied.

lit: But he was not a sheep.
sm: But he was not actually a sheep.

syn.mc: Main adversative clause.
syn.sub: This clause functions as the corrective contrast to the concessive statement in line 28.
syn.svo: Implicit subject: Christ. Verb: ἦν. Predicate nominative: πρόβατον.
syn.rhet: The rhetoric pivots from typological likeness to ontological distinction.

lit: [Even if like a voiceless lamb,]
sm: [even if he was like a silent lamb,]
emd: Editorial brackets preserved; the concessive phrase is syntactically completed by the following line.

syn.mc: Elliptical concessive phrase continued into the next line.
syn.sub: εἰ καὶ introduces a concessive structure whose adversative resolution comes in line 31.
syn.svo: Implicit subject: Christ. No expressed finite verb in this line; the idea of likeness is carried by ὡς ἀμνὸς ἄφωνος.
syn.rhet: Repeats and refines the concession of line 28, moving from πρόβατον to ἀμνός ἄφωνος.
